notes: The wrapper also runs the primary memory candidate and the report generator.
entries.paper_question: Does replay-capped memory beat the same fast transformer trained with the same clipped online SGD optimizer?
entries.paperworthiness: Required primary comparator. Without it there is no paired claim against the tuned FFN transformer.
entries.selection_rule: No selection; LR 0.15 and H=64 are inherited from the frozen protocol.
entries.title: Optimizer-matched clipped-SGD FFN

notes: H=96 gives 15617 trainable parameters, matching the H=64/P=64 memory candidate.
The current runner still emits memory rows for H=96; ignore them for this baseline.
entries.paper_question: Does the memory result survive a no-memory FFN with the same trainable parameter count as H=64/P=64 memory?
entries.paperworthiness: Required to rule out the 2080 extra prototype value parameters as the cause.
entries.selection_rule: Run the frozen LR grid on validation. Select the lowest mean validation eval_nll at 10000 steps; ties choose the smaller LR. Run only the selected config on lockbox.
entries.title: Parameter-matched FFN, H=96

notes: This is intentionally not parameter matched; it tests ordinary scale-up pressure.
entries.paper_question: Does a larger no-memory FFN close the margin under the same online data and optimizer family?
entries.paperworthiness: Required because the observed memory margins are small and could be a width effect.
entries.selection_rule: Run the frozen LR grid on validation. Select the lowest mean validation eval_nll at 10000 steps; ties choose the smaller LR. Run only the selected config on lockbox.
entries.title: Wider FFN, H=128

notes: Target replay candidate state: 26008 bytes.
entries.paper_question: Does equal learner state and temporal state-update traffic explain the memory win?
entries.paperworthiness: Required if the paper claims an algorithmic memory effect rather than a state or update-budget effect.
required_hooks: Add a baseline-only no-op state buffer that is updated every step, recorded in profiles, and never read by prediction or gradients.
entries.selection_rule: No selection; match the replay candidate state bytes exactly.
entries.title: State-matched FFN control

notes: Use benchmarks/step2_transformer_memory_throughput.py as the calibration probe, but do not use throughput numbers to retune candidate flags.
entries.paper_question: Could the FFN match or beat memory if trained for the same wall-clock budget?
entries.paperworthiness: Required because the replay path is materially slower than the FFN path.
required_hooks: Add a wrapper that reads candidate train_s per seed/horizon, runs FFN until the same wall-clock budget is consumed, and records actual steps consumed.
Separate JIT compile time from hot-loop time before matching budgets.
entries.selection_rule: Use validation to choose the FFN width/LR from the frozen grid under equal candidate train_s. Apply the selected recipe once on lockbox.
entries.title: Wall-clock-matched FFN

entries.paper_question: Is delayed replay utility necessary, or is current-token advantage enough?
entries.paperworthiness: Required to attribute the result to delayed validation-like utility rather than any advantage gate.
entries.selection_rule: No selection; this is a single preregistered mechanism ablation.
entries.title: No-replay current-token gate

notes: The current runner requires 0 < gate_max < 1, so 0.9997 is the exact preexisting practical no-cap setting.
entries.paper_question: Is the tight resource cap necessary for the memory result?
entries.paperworthiness: Required to show the positive result is not hiding an uncapped memory failure mode.
entries.selection_rule: No selection; gate_max is raised to the runner's practical no-cap value.
entries.title: No-cap replay gate

entries.paper_question: Does a fixed small residual path explain the gain without learned utility gating?
entries.paperworthiness: Required to isolate learned gate adaptation from a static residual ensemble.
entries.selection_rule: No selection; gate stays at sigmoid(-3) under the same cap.
entries.title: Fixed-gate cap-only memory

entries.paper_question: Is the reported post-FFN placement robust against placement cherry-picking?
entries.paperworthiness: Required as a secondary check. It must not replace the frozen post-FFN primary method after validation or lockbox outcomes are known.
entries.selection_rule: No selection; report the pre-FFN KV method emitted by the same runs.
entries.title: Pre-FFN KV placement check

entries.paper_question: Is the gain present in deployed memory logits, or only in the fast branch after memory-regularized training?
entries.paperworthiness: Required to state whether the mechanism is inference-time memory or training-time auxiliary regularization.
entries.selection_rule: No selection; compare eval_nll and eval_fast_nll from the same rows.
entries.title: Fast-only deployment attribution

entries.paper_question: Does the candidate need gradients from the memory loss, or only the side-channel memory/gate updates?
entries.paperworthiness: Required for training-attribution claims; secondary for the primary performance claim.
entries.selection_rule: No selection; set memory_loss_weight to 0 in blend mode.
entries.title: Fast-loss-only training ablation

entries.paper_question: Are gains due to gradient flow through the memory residual, or the nonparametric state/update path?
entries.paperworthiness: Exploratory unless the paper makes a gradient-path mechanism claim.
required_hooks: Add a flag that wraps the memory residual or prototype transform in lax.stop_gradient without changing center/gate temporal updates.
entries.title: Residual stop-gradient ablation

notes: Setting --fast-lr 0 from random initialization is not this ablation.
entries.paper_question: Can memory improve a pretrained frozen fast transformer, or does it need joint fast-path adaptation?
entries.paperworthiness: Exploratory unless the paper claims memory can be added to a fixed fast model.
required_hooks: Add checkpoint handoff from the trained FFN baseline into the memory runner.
Freeze attention, FFN, and readout after handoff while still updating centers, gate, and prototype values.
entries.selection_rule: No selection; train FFN under the primary baseline, freeze it, then train memory.
entries.title: Frozen-fast pretrained-memory ablation

lockbox_policy.direct_runner_lockbox_commands: Plan-only. Direct runner commands do not enforce lockbox access themselves; they must be launched only through a gated orchestration path.
lockbox_policy.rule: Run lockbox only after validation selection is frozen and the confirmatory decision summary has clears_confirmatory_bar exactly true. Do not alter candidate flags, baseline grids, primary metrics, horizons, seeds, or selection rules after reading validation outcomes.
lockbox_policy.validation_only_grid_rule: For grid baselines, select exactly one config on validation using the entry's selection_rule, then run that selected config once on lockbox.

Lockbox split planning is allowed only because this command is a dry run; it must not evaluate or train.
